entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud – Need APIs for Automating F...

Thank you very much.

Kind regards,

Henrike

entry.title: Linkage between Portfolio item and PPM Project
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I have requirement where PPM project is created and have to link it to portfolio item. Through blogs i got a FM /RPM/OBJECT_LINK_MODIFY but unable to use it as i am not sure what values is to be passed onto which field as Input

Any input/suggestion will be helpful.

Thanking in advance.

Sunil

entry.title: Re: SAP CPI: Not able to pick done file
entry.content: Thanks Feilipe for the in depth clarification !!

entry.title: Re: Moving into MDG career
entry.content:
As a functional consultant with experience in CRM, SD, and Ariba, you already have a strong foundation in SAP, which will be beneficial as you move into MDG (Master Data Governance). While ABAP (Advanced Business Application Programming) is not strictly mandatory for functional MDG roles, having a basic understanding of key ABAP concepts can certainly enhance your ability to work effectively with the MDG module.

Here's why ABAP knowledge can be useful:

Customization and Enhancements: MDG often involves data governance processes where you may need to customize certain aspects of the system or integrate with other SAP modules. Knowing ABAP will help you understand how to configure or extend these functionalities.

Data Processing and Interfaces: MDG typically involves working with large sets of master data, and understanding how ABAP handles data processing, transformations, and integrations can improve your problem-solving abilities in complex scenarios.

Collaboration with Technical Teams: Even if you're not directly involved in ABAP development, having a basic understanding will help you communicate more effectively with technical teams (ABAP developers) during the implementation or troubleshooting stages.

What You Need to Focus On:

Key Areas in ABAP: As a functional consultant, you don't need to dive deeply into ABAP programming, but understanding key areas like user exits, BADI (Business Add-Ins), BAPIs (Business Application Programming Interfaces), and IDocs can be highly beneficial. These are commonly used in MDG for data validation, integration, and automation.

MDG-Specific Knowledge: Focus on mastering the functional aspects of MDG, such as the data models, governance workflows, and integration with other SAP modules. ABAP knowledge will complement this, but functional expertise in MDG is essential.

entry.title: Re: ¿que día y a que hora se creo una Orden de Venta?
entry.content: Pero que muestre la hora de la creacion del pedido?, estoy mirando la tabla de LOGS ADOC y tampoco me pone la hora, sabeis que tabla almacena la fecha y HORA?

entry.title: Responses to emails by candidates not showing up in the Message Center
entry.content:
We are part of the Partneredge program, and develop integrations for our mutual customers.
One of our customers recently reported problems with our message integration, replies from their candidates weren't showing up in the Inbox.
So we got access to a demo instance of SuccessFactors HCM, in order to try and reproduce the issue.
We ran into a similar issue, were responses to emails initiated by the Message Center, dont show up their (keyword: Email Correspondence).

Overall we tried the following:
- Change the email from one of the existing candidates to "tobias.liebl@jobflow.de" (an email we have control over)
- Send a message to that user, via the candidates page
- The E-Mail is received successfully
- Reply to this E-Mail (i.e. correspondenceSFCPART002379@products57sales.successfactors.eu)
- The response doesns"t show up in the Message Center, even after waiting 20 minutes


We already checked all relevant permissions, but couldn"t find any issues.
Here is a recording explanation of the issue: https://www.loom.com/share/13f66d578d304868a25d3963786633ca

Please help me find a solution quickly, since our customer is waiting 🙂

entry.title: Re: Not getting automatic roundoff in FB60
entry.content:
Hi Ganesh,

 

For your scenario, we can post the differential amount to a separate GL. To automatically post the rounding off difference you will probably need to take help of ABAP. They will be able to develop a substitution where the GL line will be added automatically if the difference posting is less than zero.

You can set a tolerance limit for each vendor (OBA3) and user (OBA4) to ensure financial integrity

Regards,

Ashwini

entry.title: Re: Need another field in "Manage Customer Line Items" or "Manage Supplier Line Items
entry.content:
Hi @steffenriemann 

Thank you for taking the time to respond to my query and for providing valuable insights into the Manage Supplier Line Items app. I truly appreciate your effort in addressing my requirements.

The customer requires the GST Base Amount, SGST Amount, CGST Amount, and IGST Amount to be displayed in the Manage Supplier Line Items app. Currently, the TDS functionality shows the base amount and tax amount in the vendor ledger. The customer is requesting the same level of detail for CGST, SGST, and IGST, along with their corresponding base amounts. Also for this requirement am i supposed write the logic for these custom fields if yes, which BADI i have to use?

In the Data Source Extension, using the Extended Data Source FAP_VENDOR_LINE_ITEMS_SRV, I have added the following standard fields:

  1. Field Path: Supplier Line Items -> One-Time Account Data for Supplier Items -> Operational Accounting Document Item -> Additional Crcy 1 -> Additional Crcy 1
    Label: Amount in Additional Currency 1
  2. Field Path: Supplier Line Items -> One-Time Account Data for Supplier Items -> Operational Accounting Document Item -> Transaction Key
    Label: Transaction Key

However, these fields are displaying as zero in the Manage Supplier Line Items app.


Could you please provide further clarification on the full paths and context of these fields, as well as any insights into why they might be displaying zero? This will help us ensure the required GST-related fields are correctly populated and displayed.

Thank you once again for your support, and I look forward to your response.

Best regards,
Sanjay

entry.title: Report to view Unplanned Goods Issue for a maintenance Order
entry.content:
Hi,

I have come across a scenario where I need to view maintenance orders for which business has executed Unplanned Goods Issue. Kindly help me out to find out a transaction/report or tables to get the data.

Issue details - some users executed unplanned Goods Issue for maintenance orders using MB1A transaction.

I need to find out the maintenance orders for which unplanned Goods Issue is executed.

Thanks

entry.title: Re: REST API Call not working in ABAP but works in Postman
entry.content:
We had the same issue but there is a value called multivalue which is default zero. So if you pass the no. of fields you need it fetches and provides correct results. 

CALL METHOD cl_http_utility=>set_request_uri
  EXPORTING
    request    = lo_http_client->request
    uri        = lv_service
    multivalue = 3.

entry.title: Re: Move SD basic shipment to Public cloud
entry.content:
Hi @nauttawanit ,

I believe that by "SD basic shipment," you mean LE-TRA functionality, which indeed is within the compatibility scope for S/4Hana, and is not available in the Public Cloud. You can find more details in the SAP guide Transportation Management in SAP S/4HANA vs. LE-TRA.

Best regards

entry.title: Re: Introducing the New PaPM Step in Multi Actions in SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
@SarauShenthank you! It appears that my tenant didn't have that option when I first checked on tuesday, I supposed since it was a new feature or something, maybe it wasn't loaded yet correctly. Now it appears in thhe connections and I was able to make the configuration. For anyone interested in trying it out here it is a quick example.

 

You can find more information in the following blog

 

For the connection you use 

The Data Service URL Connection

https://{host}.cfapps.{region}.hana.ondemand.com/sap/opu/odata4/NXI/P1_PROCESSING

And the API data that was created on SAP BTP in your cloud foundry, for more information about how the setup to use the PaPM API, use this blog.

 

Kind Regards!

entry.title: Re: Is the Best practice for Master Data in S/4HANA Cloud...
entry.content:
Hello @yrjin 

Thank you for your message.

As of today, the SAP Best Practices for Master Data Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition are still actively maintained, but their content and versioning have evolved along with SAP's new release and documentation platforms.

You are correct that the 2208 version appears to be the last explicitly documented standalone Master Data Best Practice on some older portals. However, from release 2302 onward, master data BPs have been modularised and integrated into specific Line-of-Business (LoB) and scope item content. These can now be accessed via the SAP Signavio Process Navigator.

Where to Find the Latest Master Data Best Practices:

  • Go to: SAP Signavio Process Navigator
  • Filter by:
    • Product: SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition
    • Line of Business: Master Data or relevant LoB (e.g., Finance, Procurement)
    • Release: Choose latest (e.g., 2402)
  • Look for scope items like:
    • 1XV – Business Partner Master Data
    • 1YB – Material Master Data
    • 2LD – Product Master Data Integration

SAP Help Portal – Master Data Integration in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD

Let me know if you need further support

With regards

Chuma

entry.title: Re: Bank Payment Advice document ID generated by system S...
entry.content: Hello Harsih,

entry.title: Peer Cert Untrusted - whitelist URL
entry.content:
Hi

I have an ABAP program that calls Microsoft logon service to fetch a token. Working fine most of the time. 

But occasionally Microsoft changes the certifcate. Also i noticed that they sometimes use some load balancing with other cert. 

I know i can import cert into STRUST to fix issue - until next change on the MS side. But is there anyway where i can whitelist the url (https://login.microsoftonline.com/xxx/oauth2/v2.0/token?) so i will never get the "Untrust" issue?

entry.title: Re: Bank Payment Advice document ID generated by system S...
entry.content:
Hello Harish,

Are both the credit advice referring to the same bank account?
If they are for different bank account, I guess what I missed in my original explaination is that this number range also considers bank / bank account, Company and posting date to derive the Number ( which resets yearly).

Regards,
Harshal

entry.title: Re: SAP Standard API POST vs PATCH
entry.content:
Hello KommaShankar,

Appreciate the feedback! This post was mainly meant to give a overview of the idea of using HashSets rather than an in-depth code breakdown.

However, here’s the display queue you mentioned, hope it makes things clearer!

1) HashSet Creation using the Lookup data

Code:
Same as provided above.

Mapping & Display Queue:

2) Method Determination on received data, using the HashSets created

Code:

def String methodDet(String input, String setName, MappingContext context){
    
    HashSet myset = context.getProperty(setName);
    
    if((myset!=null)&&(myset.contains(input)))
    return "PATCH"

	return "POST" 
}

Mapping & Display Queue:

(With the Input data and SetName, it dynamically calculates the method)

But How can I use this registered device during SAP GUI Login? Or implement custom code?

Thanks
Ilian

I set do_sum for these columns and sort like that

entry.title: Re: Extract configuration from Integration Design artifacts via API

Other colleague pointed me to this blog:

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/cpi-scheduling-monitoring/ba-p/13422636

Which I am currently exploring. With a small adaptation in the groovy script it works for Cloud Foundry and is able to extract the data from the tenant.

Javier

entry.title: Re: Good Receipt Production Embedded EWM

entry.title: Replication of non working hours to SAP S4HANA Service "Private" using SAP CPI
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I hope this message finds you well.

We are currently working on a project involving the integration of SAP Field Service Management (FSM) with SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition, using SAP Cloud Integration (CPI), and we have a few questions we would appreciate your insights on:

  1. Is it possible to replicate non-productive hours from SAP FSM to S/4HANA?

  2. If so, how can this be achieved, and what would this data generate or trigger within S/4HANA?

  3. Would this replication have any impact on the CATS (Cross-Application Time Sheet) module?

Additionally, if there is any official documentation or reference material that describes this process in detail, it would be greatly appreciated if you could share it.

Best regards,

#FSM #SAPFIELDSERVICEMANAGEMENT #SAPFSM

entry.title: Re: SAP learning journey
entry.content:
Hello,

you understood something wrong:

Core sytem s/4 database always hana, if core system ERP, can have hana db.

On prem or cloud.

GUI: SAPGui, WebDynpro, Fiori. Fiori is  the newest.

SAP BW => Has Hana DB or not. Cloud BW = SAP Datasphere. Important: BW is an extra sytem. A s/4 or ERP runs without BW.

You should orientate at what do you find at work.

If you do not have or use a BW why learn about it. 

SAC is also an extra system which works like a super GUI. But the data comes from a backend system (s/4 or BW or something else).

Same as BW. Do you have one or not?

entry.title: Re: convert nested JSON to ABAP
entry.content: As an alternative, have a look at this also: https://github.com/sbcgua/ajson

entry.title: SAC previous year quantity calculation by week
entry.content:
Hello everyone

I have an date input control as a calendar, and I have a table that shows the quantities sold in the chosed year divided by weeks, and this is fine.

Now I want to show, week by week, also the quantities sold in the previous year in that particolar week (example if I see the week 17 in the 2025, I want to show close to this value , the quantity sold in the week 17 for the 2024).

If I try to implement this , SAC doesn't show me the value of the column of the week of the previous year.

If I try to set the hierarchy starting from month, I can see the quantity sold in the same month of the previous year, but if I try to drill down I get this error

Is there a way to achieve the result? And how?

Thank you for your reply

Adriano

entry.title: Re: c+048 error occurs repeatedly in public cloud
entry.content: I found out through SAP case that the problem was caused by the data update not being continuous in some related tables. However, SAP said that I should solve it through case every time the problem occurs. The problem is that this case occurs frequently.

entry.title: Re: SAP learning journey
entry.content:
Hi @JanSchlichting,

 

Thank you for the clarification! I really appreciate your input, it definitely helps me better understand the landscape. I’ll adjust my learning path accordingly.

Thanks again,

Nielsen

entry.title: My Inbox PO Link Opens ME23N Instead of Fiori App "Manage Purchase Orders"
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

 

I hope someone can help me or guide me on this issue I'm currently analyzing.

 

In My Inbox, when clicking the Purchase Order (PO) number link then clicking Manage Purchase Orders, it currently opens the SAP GUI transaction ME23N. However, the requirement is to open the Fiori app "Manage Purchase Orders" instead, meaning it will open the details page of this specific Purchase Order number

 

 

I was advised to check the semantic object configuration in the CDS or how the link is generated in the detail page. I also checked the network calls, and it seems the data is coming from a CDS with prefix C_ or possibly I_.

 

My questions are:

 

Where can I check or change the navigation target for the PO link in My Inbox?

 

Is it possible to redirect the navigation to the "Manage Purchase Orders" Fiori app using intent-based navigation?

 

Should this be done via CDS annotation (e.g., @Consumption.semanticObject) or through Fiori app extensions?

 

Has anyone done a similar implementation or redirection from ME23N to a Fiori app?

 

Any help or suggestions would be greatly appreciated!

 

Thank you in advance!

entry.title: Re: Move SD basic shipment to Public cloud
entry.content:
Hello @nauttawanit 

Assuming that by "SD basic shipment" you mean Transportation (LE-TRA), it is not available in S/4HANA Public Cloud. It has been replaced with Transportation Management (TM), which is a gutted version of Transportation Management (TM) in S/4HANA Private Cloud / on-premise.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: How to add an item to the import function
entry.content:
How to add a cost center item to the import function for free shipments in the App Import Sales Orders Without Charge (App ID: F6382)


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to add an item to the import function
entry.content:
Please refer to the following links:

  • Import Sales Orders Without Charge - You can only import the fields (including required and optional ones) listed in the template. How to add an optional field to the document data sheet?
  • App Extensibility: Import Sales Orders Without Charge

If the standard field which you require is not present as per the first link, please consider adopting the extensibility workaround listed in the second link or alternatively raise this requirement via the SAP Influence program

entry.title: Re: Multitenant App is not able to load $metadata of backend service (401 Unauthorized)
entry.content: The configuration without destination service should work. If the token is forwarded to your backend application you need to investigate what target application does with it. This depends on the stack: for Java+Spring Boot you need to trace Spring Security, for Node.js you need to trace the appropriate middleware.

entry.title: Re: SAP Standard API POST vs PATCH
entry.content: Thank you!!

entry.title: Re: 🧰👷‍♂️ The SAP S/4HANA RISE & SAP BTP - Toolbox 👷‍♂️🧰
entry.content:
SAP FS-PE Payment Engine

this is an old picture and a useful one Payment Engine (FS-PE) | SAP Help Portal

 

Admin Help: 

3015435 - SAP FS-PE: Inquiry is solved by SAP Help documentation - SAP for Me

SAP Payment Engine | SAP Help Portal

SAP_Payment_Engine_90_EN.pdf

PAM:

SAP Product Availability Matrix

FS-PE is out of maintenance now in 2025

to find out the future of it, go here:  Availability Matrices for Focused Business Solutions

and search for Payment Engine and click the link:

Release Availability Documentation

then click the Compatibility Scope link:

2269324 - Compatibility Scope Matrix for SAP S/4HANA - SAP for Me

then go to the attachments and click the link to the Excel

which will then tell you that alternative is:

SAP S/4HANA Banking for payment centralization | SAP Help Portal

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_BANKING_FOR_PAYMENT_CENTRALIZATION

About This Guide | SAP Help Portal

SAP_PE_Admin_Guide.pdf PE 9.0

Admin Guide About This Guide | SAP Help Portal

Product Assistance SAP S/4HANA Banking for payment centralization | SAP Help Portal

Technical Concepts Technical Concepts | SAP Help Portal

Useful Links Useful Links | SAP Help Portal

1405042 - Release Strategy for the Add-on PAY-ENGINE - SAP for Me

Integrations and Connecting AM and other systems Connection of Internal and External Systems | SAP Help Portal

 

 

 

Here is the conversion guide for ECC S/4HANA from Payment Engine to the upgrade S/4HANA Banking for Payment Centralisation 4adf4a00391d468eb937eb743099e70f.pdf

Payment Engine to Payment Centralisation is an Upgrade...  https://help.sap.com/doc/775ed032ba984c76b28d9c32c4c18f7c/2007.05/en-US/SAP_PE_Admin_Guide.pdf

Payment Transaction Processing:  Payment_Transaction_Processing.pdf

2628981 - SOA: Data Types for business object PaymentTransactionOrder - SAP for Me

2628950 - SOA: Data Types for business object PaymentTransactionMessage - SAP for Me

https://help.sap.com/doc/775ed032ba984c76b28d9c32c4c18f7c/2007.05/en-US/SAP_PE_Admin_Guide.pdfSAP_Payment_Engine_90_EN.pdf

 

 

SAP_PE_Admin_Guide.pdf

The PAM for Payment Centralisation is here:  SAP Product Availability Matrix

Maintenance Information is here:  Availability Matrices for Focused Business Solutions

2269324 - Compatibility Scope Matrix for SAP S/4HANA - SAP for Me

Release Availability Documentation

Payment Centralization is supported including S/4HANA 2023 and up to 2030

Microsoft Word - RAD_S4paymentcentralization_November2023.docx

and Evolving in a digital world with Banking as a Service | SAP Fioneer is mentioned

 

Connecting to the AM Account Management System

Connection to an Account Management System | SAP Help Portal

Product Assistance for Payment Centralisation SAP_Payment_Engine_90_EN.pdf

 

This is the roadmap...   Advanced Payment Management | SAP

S/4HANA Cloud for Advanced Payment Management

entry.content:

we have two issues that we would like to adjust in the learning library view. Firstly, the word "Estimated" next to the price should be removed besides the price as it causes confusion for users. In addition, we no longer want to display the description of the item in the library. We only want to display the title in the library without any descriptions.

Are there any possibilities to realize this?

Thank you!

Best regards

Anna

entry.title: Re: Queries on CLT: Myanmar - GL settings
entry.content:
Hi Duarte,

Answer to 2nd question:

If your question about Germany's activation pertains to whether it is necessary or a prerequisite for Myanmar's activation in the system, the answer is NO. Germany can be activated in the system independently of Myanmar's activation or rollout, based on the business needs.

Regards

Sathya N

entry.title: Re: Extract configuration from Integration Design artifacts via API
entry.content:
Hey Javier,

Just for the sake of completeness I will post a brief update: based on the the script Ariel wrote, it looks like he was able to find the parser which can be used for quartz definitions.

So he just searches for the quartz component definitions (that xml stuff I showed above) from the blueprint (meaning runtime, rather than design time - it will only work after you press "deploy") and then calculates next runs (using exactly the CronTrigger stuff we found above in quartz docs).

Basically, this function does the magic, so maybe you can just try using the parser yourself.

After all, as you can see this (from blueprint)

?cron=0+0/5+*+?+*+MON,WED,TUE,THU,FRI+*

is the same as this (from api for advanced schedule minus the --tz --startAt and --endAt)

0 0/5 * ? * MON,WED,TUE,THU,FRI *

And he replaces pluses with spaces while you already have it.

With "basic" mode probably runtime camel blueprint is the only way to get proper cron definition.

Regards,

Alex

entry.title: Re: How to copy HDI container with data from production t...
entry.content:
Hi Pierre, 
you can copy HDI container, that are larger than 2 GB, by export to and import from cloud storage:
Export an SAP HDI Container for Copy Purposes to a Cloud Store 
Import an SAP HDI Container for Copy Purposes from a Cloud Store 
Importing and Exporting Data (chose your preferred cloud storage provider)
You can either export/import to/from your own cloud storage (like an S3 bucket) or you can use the BTP Object Store service. 
There is also a nice blog on this topic: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/export-import-an-sap-hdi-container-for-copy-purposes-to-a-cloud-store/ba-p/13559291
As the blog was published nearly two years ago, some details may have changed (for instance there is typically no need anymore to import cloud storage certificates, as these are already provided with latest SAP HANA Cloud releases). Therefore I would recommend to double check always the product documentation. 
Best regards, 
  Alexander

entry.title: Re: Step-by-Step Guide to Setting Up Joule with SAP SuccessFactors
entry.content:
Hi Chetann_Nagpal ,

Please note that according to our Rules of Engagement: "If you want to publish content that was helped/created by GenAI, you must add the user tag  GenAI Assisted Content." The GenAI user tag has been added as we detected AI-generated content in your post. Thank you for your contribution in the SAP Community!

Best regards,

 Kavya

entry.title: Re: Service Contract contract end date default
entry.content:
hi Soma,

I just want to point out that there is a negative performance impact when you set the service contract end date very far in the future.
Billing plan will be created for the entire runtime of the contract and all those billing request lines will run through pricing every time you open , edit or change the service contract.


If possible we would suggest to use automatic renewal if possible to avoid performance issues.

Best regards Stephanie

entry.title: SuccessFactors story table issues
entry.content:
Hi

Just wanted to see if this is a common issue. We get a lot of error messages but this one is slightly different.

I'm using Stories in SuccessFactors. When I go into a Story that has a table sometimes the table doesn't load correctly. There are no error messages but you can tell because the fields in the table are completely different. The first column is usually the Count field which you normally get when you first add a table. Then it seems to have a lot of other fields that you didn't put into the table. And when you go to the Builder Section, it indicates there are no fields in the table despite the table being huge.

Previously I've rebuilt the table but now it seems you just have to leave the Story and come back to it another time, which is not ideal. I guess I'm wondering if this is a connection issue in some way?

I've included a screenshot of the table deliberately using a section where the other fields are empty. There is data there, just didn't want to put it in the screenshot. Is this a common problem and if so does anyone know the cause?

Thanks

entry.title: Uploading Attachments via API
entry.content:
I am trying to upload a document, and attach it to a user's profile. The upload is successful and can be retrieved from the API using its ID, however I cannot find the file in the document manager, nor elsewhere in the UI. 

I have attached the sample body i am sending here:

im performing this action via the /odata/v2/Attachment endpoint

Thanks for any help

Thanks,

entry.title: Re: Issue while activating of Advanced Foreign Currency Valuation

entry.title: SAP Note #2502552に記載のノートについて
entry.content:
SAPご担当者様

お世話になります。
現在、S/4HANAアップグレード検討の準備として、SAP Readiness Checkを実行したいと考えています。
Readiness Checkで実行するシンプル化項目整合性チェック有効化の調査をしているのですが、以下ノートについて追加で確認させてください。

DIGITS - S4TC - SAP S/4HANA 変換およびアップグレード新規シンプル化項目チェック


＜ご質問＞

SAP Note #DIGITSについて

Q1. SAP Note #DIGITSの"ソリューション"項目の中に、「TCI に対する追加修正 (自動的に取り込まれる)」と書かれているノートが8件あります。これらのノートが"自動的に取り込まれる"とはどういう意味でしょうか？

また、Q1で前述した8件のノートは、SAP Note #DIGITSの前提条件であると認識しています。その中のひとつSAP Note #DIGITSの前提条件にSAP Note #DIGITSがあります。
このSAP Note #DIGITSについて以下2点確認させてください。

SAP Note #DIGITSについて

Q2."現象"や"理由および前提条件"項目に、"SAP S/4HANA DIGITS リリース"の記載があるのですが、こちらのノートはSAP S/4HANA DIGITS リリースへのアップグレード時のみ対象でしょうか？それ以降のリリースへのアップグレード時も対象ですか？

 

Q3.このノートの注意点の一つに「当 SAP ノートは、SAP S/4HANA 出力制御を使用していないアプリケーションには関連しません。」とあります。今回のアップグレード対象システムでは使用していないソリューションなのではないかと思っているのですが、SAP S/4HANA 出力制御を使用していない場合、このノートは適用不可・不要でしょうか？また、SAP S/4HANA 出力制御の使用有無についての確認方法はありますか？

＜追加情報＞
現行S/4HANA本番機の関連ソフトウェアコンポーネントのリリース：
SAP_BASIS    751 SP02
S4CORE        101 SP01


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: SAP Note #2502552に記載のノートについて
entry.content:
＞Q1. SAP Note #2502552の"ソリューション"項目の中に、「TCI に対する追加修正 (自動的に取り込まれる)」と書かれているノートが8件あります。これらのノートが"自動的に取り込まれる"とはどういう意味でしょうか？ 

→SNOTE -> In note browser -> select the note 2502552 -> Menu Goto -> Analysis Note ->Display prerequisites SAP notes より、前提のノートがシステムの状況より自動的に表示されます。
念のため、画面キャプチャを添付いたします。お手数ですが、ご参照になれば幸いです。


Q2とQ3はコンポーネントCA-GTF-OCの担当範囲です。弊社ポリシー（ノート50048）により、新規ケースで対応する必要がございます。
お客様のエフォートを減少するために、こちらよりそのコンポーネントも専門家と連携して確認できた結果をご報告いたします。
もし引き続き何か追加質問があれば、お手数ですが、弊社ポリシーに基づいて、コンポーネントCA-GTF-OCへ新規ケースをオープンしてご対応いただきますようお願いいたします。
ご理解いただけますと幸いです。

＞Q2."現象"や"理由および前提条件"項目に、"SAP S/4HANA 2021 リリース"の記載があるのですが、こちらのノートはSAP S/4HANA 2021 リリースへのアップグレード時のみ対象でしょうか？
＞それ以降のリリースへのアップグレード時も対象ですか？ 

→SAP S/4HANA 2021およびそれ以降のリリースへのアップグレード時も対象でございます。

＞Q3.このノートの注意点の一つに「当 SAP ノートは、SAP S/4HANA 出力制御を使用していないアプリケーションには関連しません。」とあります。
＞今回のアップグレード対象システムでは使用していないソリューションなのではないかと思っているのですが、SAP S/4HANA 出力制御を使用していない場合、
＞このノートは適用不可・不要でしょうか？

→はい、ご認識の通りです。上記の場合、本ノートの適用は不要です。

＞また、SAP S/4HANA 出力制御の使用有無についての確認方法はありますか？ 
→ 3097507 - Output Management in SAP S/4HANA On-Premise の添付ファイルCurrent status of business applications.xlsxを確認していただければ、
全てのアプリケーションの使用状態が確認できます。
制御を調整するなら、SPRO -> CROSS-APPLICATION COMPONENTS -> OUTPUT CONTROL -> MANAGE APPLICATION OBJECT TYPE ACTIVATIONで管理することができます。

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Logistics Private Cloud Edition FAQ & Knowledge Base Series Compilation
entry.content:
Over the past few years, the Product Management team for SAP S/4HANA Logistics private cloud edition has published several articles, each one dealing with the most frequently asked questions on a range of topics. To facilitate access to the information and guidance we provide, this blog post will compile our FAQ articles into a single source to make tracking these articles easier as we release them.

The focus of our blog posts is to provide insightful articles related to warehouse and transportation management solutions in SAP S/4HANA private cloud edition, as well as integrated solutions such as Artificial Intelligence, which are released regularly.

  1. Articles with essential product knowledge and sources to help get projects running:
    1. SAP Extended Warehouse Management, Private Cloud Edition
    2. SAP Transportation Management in SAP S/4HANA, Private Cloud Edition
  2. SAP EWM FAQ Series:
    1. Support Tools
      • Overview of Support & Guidance Tools
      • The ABAP Test Cockpit Checks for SAP EWM
      • Overview of the SAP EWM Check Monitor
      • How to Setup Monitoring & Alerts
    2. EWM clean core strategies and future roadmap
    3. System Architecture
    4. SCM Optimizer for EWM
    5. Release Strategy for SAP’s Warehouse Management
  3. Knowledge from Developers:
    1. Production Integration
      •  Staging to Production in SAP Extended Warehouse Management - Overview
      • Receiving from Production in SAP Extended Warehouse Management - Overview
      • Guide on How to Configure Distribution Equipment in EWM
    2. Material Flow Systems
      • SAP EWM MFS Communication
      • SAP EWM MFS Communication Customizing & Master Data
      • SAP EWM MFS Receiving and Processing Telegrams
      • Sending MFS Telegrams
      • SAP EWM MFS Reliable Communication - System Supervision of Connections
    3. RF Framework
      • Working with RF Transactions in the Warehouse
      • Even more Flexibility in Adaptation using Screen Personas Scripting with Mobile RF Devices in Warehouse Management
  4. SAP Consulting Solutions
    1. Real-Time Locating System for SAP Extended Warehouse Management
    2. SimpleFioris@EWM
    3. PM(EAM)-EWM Integration
    4. Missing Parts for SAP Extended Warehouse Management
  5. Premium Hub CoE – DSC Knowledge Bits Series
  6. Series of Transportation Management Knowledge Base Articles from Michael Haase

FIND OUT MORE!

You can also find more technical details and documentation on the above listed topic and others we left out via the “What’s New” viewer on the Help Documentation portal.

For more information about SAP Extended Warehouse Management, please follow us on social media, our YouTube channel or our community pages:

SAP EWM Community

SAP YouTube Channel

EWM LinkedIn Community

SAP Extended Warehouse Management Roadmap Explorer

Release information and restrictions of embedded EWM on SAP S/4HANA 2023

Release information and restrictions of Decentralized EWM on SAP S/4HANA 2023

For a complete list of Q&A from the EWM community, please access this link:
https://answers.sap.com/tags/01200615320800000705

In case you do not find your specific question there, feel free to post your question via the following form:
https://answers.sap.com/questions/ask.html?primaryTagId=01200615320800000705

entry.title: Re: sap ibp net requirement - simple calculation without...
entry.content: To make it clear :

entry.title: Re: SAF-T for Romania, adding additional fields in Analyt...
entry.content:
Hi Fabio,

in case of RO SAF-T, analyze data is provided only on the line item level (<nsSAFT:InvoiceLine>). Invoice header information (<nsSAFT:Invoice>), analysis types (<nsSAFT:Analysis>) and taxes (<nsSAFT:TaxInformation>) are not included. 

For verification of the xml content it is recommended to always check the xml directly. You can also check the data preview, which should have all the data reported in the xml.

I would not recommend to create a copy of the cds views and change the analyze data queries. You can extend the cds views (both C and Q views should be extended) to add more fields to the current queries: EXTEND VIEW ENTITY

Also, the queries are replaced with C_RO_SAFTNonResidentSlsInvcC/C_RO_SAFTNonResidentSlsInvcQ and C_RO_SAFTNonResidentPurInvcC/C_RO_SAFTNonResidentPurInvcQ, so these should be enhanced if required after the latest changes to the report are applied, not the old ones.

Best regards,

Gergely

entry.title: SAP ALM Summit APAC 2025
entry.content:
Join us for the SAP ALM Summit APAC, a 3-day event designed to enhance your knowledge and skills in Application Lifecycle Management. Immerse yourself in a world of innovation and collaboration as you network with experts from the industry during our exciting keynote session on the first day. The second day brings plenary sessions featuring multiple parallel expert discussions, along with the thrilling ALMathon and networking dinner. On the third day, dive into hands-on sessions and put your expertise to the test with the highly anticipated Solution Transformation Consultant Badge. Don't miss this opportunity to learn, network, and grow.

 

Register Now

entry.title: Re: sap ibp net requirement - simple calculation without...
entry.content: To make it clear | Metric | March | April | May | June | July | August | September | October | November | December | |------------|-------|-------|------|------|------|--------|-----------|---------|----------|----------| | PROJINVT0 | 150 | | | | | | | | | | | PROJINV | 150 | 130 | 90 | 120 | 90 | 60 | 40 | 65 | 40 | 40 | | DMD | | 20 | 40 | 80 | 30 | 30 | 20 | 30 | 25 | 60 | | SS | | 10 | 20 | 40 | 15 | 15 | 10 | 15 | 12.5 | 30 | | DOS | | 60 | 120 | 110 | 60 | 50 | 50 | 55 | 85 | 60 | | PROJSELLOUT| | 0 | 0 | 110 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 55 | 0 | 60 | PROJINVOFFSET = IBP_PERIODSHIFT(PROJINV,1) PROJINT = PROJINVOFFSET-( SS +DMD) PROJSELLOUT = IF(PROJINT<0,0,MAX(PROJINT;DOS)) PROJINV = IF(PERIODID3 =$$PERIODID3CU$$-1,PROJINVT0,PROJSELLOUT +IBP_PROJINVOFFSET-DMD)

entry.title: different e-mail adresses for sending purchase order and consignment settlement
entry.content:
We would like to send the consignment settlement message (output type KONS) to our supplier via e-mail to a different e-mail adress than the purchase order. 

In SAP standard I found no possibility to manage that. As described in SAP Help we use the standard programm RM08NAST with form ENTRY_KONS_PDF. 

The program is always taking the standard e-mail adress from business partner and I have no possibility to change it.  Is there any idea how we can send the message to another recipient?

entry.title: OData $batch Request Limitation in SAP Build Apps
entry.content:
I’m trying to update multiple items in SAP S/4HANA using an OData $batch request from  SAP Build Apps . I generate the correct multipart/mixed payload with boundaries in JavaScript. However, when sending this via the HTTP Destination Request, I get the error:
"Invalid body type for binary request. Please provide a file object."

  • SAP Build Apps only accepts a File object (from a File Picker) for binary bodies—not a programmatically created Blob or string.

  • Other body types (JSON, x-www-form-urlencoded, multipart/form-data) are not compatible with OData $batch, which strictly requires multipart/mixed.

  • This blocks seamless, programmatic OData batch operations—users would have to manually upload a file, which breaks UX.

Question:
Has anyone found a workaround for this? Is there a way to send a multipart/mixed OData $batch request from SAP Build Apps without requiring a File Picker or user-uploaded file? Or is this a known limitation with any planned fix?




Additional information:
JS createBatchPayload:

const records = inputs.input1; // List of selected items
const newGroup = inputs.input2; // e.g., "101", "102", etc.

const batchBoundary = "batch_test";
const changesetBoundary = "changeset_test";

// Log inputs
console.log(" createBatchPayload.js");
console.log(" Records:", JSON.stringify(records, null, 2));
console.log(" New Purchasing Group:", newGroup);

// Safety check
if (!records || !Array.isArray(records) || records.length === 0) {
console.warn(" No records provided to batch payload.");
return { result: "" };
}

if (!newGroup || typeof newGroup !== "string" || newGroup.trim() === "") {
console.warn(" Missing or invalid Purchasing Group input.");
return { result: "" };
}

let batchParts = [];

records.forEach((item, index) => {
const patchBlock = [
`--${changesetBoundary}`,
"Content-Type: application/http",
"Content-Transfer-Encoding: binary",
`Content-ID: ${index + 1}`,
"",
`PATCH PurchaseReqnItem(PurchaseRequisition='${item.Document}',PurchaseRequisitionItem='${item.DocumentItem}') HTTP/1.1`,
"Content-Type: application/json",
"If-Match: *",
"",
JSON.stringify({ PurchasingGroup: newGroup }),
""
].join(" ");

batchParts.push(patchBlock);
});

const payload = [
`--${batchBoundary}`,
`Content-Type: multipart/mixed; boundary=${changesetBoundary}`,
"",
...batchParts,
`--${changesetBoundary}--`,
`--${batchBoundary}--`
].join(" ");

console.log("✅ Final Batch Payload: ", payload);

return { result: payload };

JS converToBlob:

// Input: outputs.createBatchPayload.result (text payload)
// Output: Blob ready for HTTP request

const payloadText = inputs.input1;
const boundary = "batch_test";

console.log(" convertToBlob.js");
console.log(" Input payload length:", payloadText.length);

// Validate payload structure
if (!payloadText.includes(`--${boundary}`)) {
console.error(" Missing batch boundary markers");
return { blob: null };
}

// Create Blob with correct MIME type
const blob = new Blob([payloadText], {
type: `multipart/mixed; boundary=${boundary}`
});

console.log(" Created Blob:", {
size: blob.size,
type: blob.type
});

// Optional verification
const reader = new FileReader();
reader.onload = () => {
console.log(" Blob content sample:", reader.result.substring(0, 200));
};
reader.readAsText(blob);

return { blob: blob };

entry.title: IM Cycle count with limit to max no.of items per Physical Inventory Document
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

Currently we have a batch job running once every week for the IM cycle counting (MICN)

Issue: System creates multiple Physical Inventory docs with 20 items (Max no. of items) each not stopping at one document once the job runs for that week. This way it creates many Physical Inventory docs to be counted in a week which the client doesn't require.

Expected : Only one physical inventory document is to be created with 20 items (Max no. of items given in the variant - MICN selection screen).

Eg - 110 items to be counted with CC indicators (A,B,C,D)

PI doc 1 - 20 items
PI doc 2 - 20 items
PI doc 3 - 20 items
PI doc 4 - 20 items
PI doc 5 - 20 items
PI doc 6 - 10 items

Expected is to create only PI doc 1  - 20 items and stop.

Do we have any resolution for this in the standard ?

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Need another field in "Manage Customer Line Items" or "Manage Supplier Line Items
entry.content:
Hello @Sanjay_BS1 

Thank you for providing the details of the fields you added. 

The field path includes the association to the entity "One-Time Account Data for Supplier Items". This entity is filled only for postings on a one-time-account supplier. Entity "One-Time Account Data for Supplier Items" is supposed to store the supplier data like name, address, and bank data for a one-time-account supplier. 

When you post on a "normal" supplier this data segment is not needed and empty. In such a case, also any further association from the entity "One-Time Account Data for Supplier Items" would empty as well. You can test these fields for a posting to a one-time-account supplier and then the additional fields would be filled.

Now, fields like "Amount in Additional Currency 1" you can already access in the standard application. Please look for fields with labels "Amt in Add. Crcy 1" or "Amt in Add. Crcy 2". These fields are already active and you don't need to use data source extension for it.

Field "Transaction Key", however, is a rather special field and it is used to determine accounts or posting keys for line items which are created automatically by the system. This field is not part of the standard fields of the app Manage Supplier Line Items and cannot be added via Extensibility. If you feel, you need this field in this app, please send a feature request to SAP and provide some background for the use case.

Best regards
Steffen

entry.title: Candidates are receiving multiple repeating disqualification emails - Recruiting Management
entry.content:
Candidates are receiving multiple repeating disqualification emails - Recruiting Management

This happens after they have been in Hiring Manager disqualified status and are later moved to a different disqualifier status. 
Hiring Manager disqualified status has only an infomail for the recruiter but no candidate email.

I found this KBA 3562711 - Candidates are receiving multiple repeating disqualification emails - Recruiting Management
Though I am not sure if this is alreay patched in my instance has this happend for someone else? 

Does anybody have an idea what could be the issue behind this? We expierenced the repeating emails mostly during the last week. Thanks

entry.title: SAP CC allowance counters
entry.content:
Hi Team,

I would like to know in a scenario where we need to store values for multiple periods (for e.g. monthly counters) using allowances, what would be the recommended way of handling this? Manage multiple counters within a single allowance or create separate allowances by having less counters per allowance.

Regards,

Aravind

entry.title: Re: Issue while activating of Advanced Foreign Currency V...
entry.content:
Dear @R_Pooja ,

first of all thanks a lot for showing interest in the Advanced Valuations and raising these important questions. Let me try to answer them. 

  1. You are right, the Classic Valuations will be deprecated in a future release for Public Cloud. The announcement made some releases ago that release CE2508 will be the target release for the deprecation is obsolet. This information was provided via 'What's New' as well. A dedicated release cannot yet be announced. Once a target release for the deprecation is appointed, we need to inform customers at least one release earlier. 
  2. SAP recommends to start with the Advanced Valuations with the beginning of a new fiscal year. The recommendation is because from our point and due to auditing reasons and purposes we would not mix up two different valuation processes in one fiscal year. If customers would like to change in the middle of the year, there is no technical restriction to do so. For such scenarios, SAP recommends to align with the auditors first.
  3. The Advanced Valuations can be tested in test- and/or quality-systems. If you would like to deactivate them again, you need to reverse all postings created by the Advanced Valuations as a first step. Subsequently, a ticket need to be raised. Based on this, we help to circumvent the error message while trying to uncheck the "Activate Advanced Valuation" checkbox. 

Thanks again for raising these questions. 

Kind regards,

Kevin

entry.title: Administrator's rights on application losts
entry.content:
Hi,

On my development platform, i'm making some tests about the autorizations, and I seem to have found something odd to me. 

In CMC -> Application -> User Sécurity, It seems I'm able to revoke definitively the access right from the Administrator user doing this:

The following screen is:

And then, the Crystal Report app is not even listed in the menu, and I can't restore the rights, even with the administrator user. I confirmed this behaviour on BOBI 4.2SP9P11 and BOBI 4.3SP4P8.

Is it working as expected? Is there a way to restore those rights?

entry.title: Re: Hi PCOE mates
entry.content:
Hi Luis,
PCOE (Partner COE) is addressed to SAP Partners, Customer COE is the expert team on the SAP customer side. It is therefore a different view.
For partner-specific questions, I would be happy to refer you to colleagues in Partner Management. Please send me a short email to annette.michel@sap.com. Many thanks, Annette

entry.title: Update Sales Order Price with Supplier Invoice Price in 3rd party process
entry.content:
Hi,

My client has a 3rd party process where they have a "bill-through" scenario.
Is there a way to automatically update the sales order price if the amount on the supplier invoice doesn't match the original purchase order price?

I can't seem to find a solution for public cloud.

Thanks!

entry.title: Re: Hi PCOE mates
entry.content:
Thanks Annette, I'll send to you an email. 

Regards,
Lluis

entry.title: Re: Pro-code UI Extension in SAP Sales and Service Cloud V2
entry.content:
hello,

Thank you for the feedback!

An additional point, is there any specific additional set up due to mash up error 'embedding blocked because the allowlist or the allow list service is not configured correctly'?

Thank you in advanced

Best regadrs

entry.title: Re: Supplying Export parameters as Import parameters to a Remote Enabled Function Module
entry.content:
Usually, you don't need to deal with low level things like RfcCreateStructure() yourself, this is done automatically for you, when you create the "Function".

But I think, I now understand what's going wrong here: ABAP is case-insensitive, while programming languages like Python and C are case-sensitive. Therefore, specifying the RFM parameters in lower-case works in your ABAP report, but when you try the same in your Python client code, it doesn't recognize the function module parameter name ("RFC_INVALID_PARAMETER").

If this is the problem here, then changing your Python code sample to upper-case should fix it:

params = {
            'IT_CODE': val1,
            'IV_REPORT_NAME': val2,
            'IV_DEVCLASS': val3
        }

(Try it first with plain CHAR-type parameters. -- Structures will then be the next step, once this works...)

entry.title: Re: CPI to CPI Connection and both tenants will be on different subaccounts at different regions.
entry.content:
Hi @Yogita1 ,

With JMS adapter you can't because it is for local JMS queues in the same tenant.

With XI protocol maybe... but I don't see the advantage. Personally, I don't like XI protocol, it is like an old protocol from PI/PO for on-prem integration engines...

Regards,

Roberto

entry.title: Re: edit a job history by import without inserting a new...
entry.content: Hello, Please try to import job history with the sequence number field.

entry.title: Maximize your SAP Investment through SAP Cloud ALM: Implementation & Operation
entry.content:
Hello All, 

SAP Cloud ALM Awareness Sessions, we call this session "Maximize your SAP Investment through SAP Cloud ALM: Implementation & Operation"

Who should attend 

​Customers who are at the beginning of their SAP Cloud ALM journey with little to no experience in this area

Session Goal

​Provide a first understanding of what SAP Cloud ALM offers, understand its significance, and explore ways to onboard

Agenda: 

  • Introduction to SAP Cloud ALM
  • SAP Cloud ALM accelerates implementation
  • SAP Cloud ALM safeguards operations 
  • SAP Cloud ALM enables Clean Core 
  • Transition to SAP Cloud ALM  

Register Now

entry.title: Re: Bank Payment Advice document ID generated by system S...

entry.title: Maximize your SAP Investment through SAP Cloud ALM: Implementation & Operation
entry.content:
Hello All, 

SAP Cloud ALM Awareness Sessions, we call this session "Maximize your SAP Investment through SAP Cloud ALM: Implementation & Operation"

Who should attend 

​Customers who are at the beginning of their SAP Cloud ALM journey with little to no experience in this area

Session Goal

​Provide a first understanding of what SAP Cloud ALM offers, understand its significance, and explore ways to onboard

Agenda: 

  • Introduction to SAP Cloud ALM
  • SAP Cloud ALM accelerates implementation
  • SAP Cloud ALM safeguards operations 
  • SAP Cloud ALM enables Clean Core 
  • Transition to SAP Cloud ALM  

Register Now

entry.title: Upgrading from SAP Build community tier, I need help!
entry.content: I am seeking guidance on transitioning from the SAP Build community tier to a higher-tier plan to accommodate team collaboration.

entry.title: Onboarding - Review New Hire Data - Assign Recruiter
entry.content:
Hi,

we trigger the Onboarding processes in Recruiting and would like to make sure that the Review New Hire Data step, the first step in Onboarding, will be assigned automatically to the recruiter. Does anyone of you know if this can be achieved and if yes - how?

Thank you for your help!

Kind regards,

Adiran

entry.title: How to install DTW on client/workstation PC's with SAP Business One 10.0
entry.content:
I need to use DTW on client PCs for daily tasks.
I checked b1_shr and did not find DTW's setup.exe.
I checked the SAP Business One Administrator's Guide but didn't find any useful information. Should I install it using the complete B1 installation package? But how can I install only DTW?

We are using SAP Business One 10.0 (10.00.240) SP 2402 (Security) HOTFIX1 (64-bit) for windows.

entry.title: Re: ALV Subtotal
entry.content:
The REUSE_ALV_GRID_DISPLAY_LVC FM uses the CL_GUI_ALV_GRID class.

Handle the AFTER_REFRESH event of this class to update the totals/sub-totals using the GET_SUBTOTALS method and trigger a new soft display using the REFRESH_TABLE_DISPLAY method.

  • Caution: Use a flag in the AFTER_REFRESH handling method to avoid an infinite loop
  • Why did you use the FM and not the class?

entry.title: Building Trustworthy SAP UI5 with AI governance of watsonx.gov
entry.content:
Introduction

Governance is crucial to ensuring ethical AI use when businesses incorporate Generative AI (GenAI) into SAP-based customer care systems. The integration of AI assistants with SAP UI5 applications, which are frequently utilised for end-user portals, support dashboards, and service desks, offers concerns associated with the exposure of personal data, erroneous responses, and non-compliance with guidelines.

As the governance layer, GenAI Governance makes sure the AI assistant operates morally, safely, and in accordance with company guidelines. IBM watsonx.governance offers strong supervision for AI models used in UI5 apps when it is connected with SAP BTP.

 

Importance of AI Governance in AI applications

Governance is essential for AI applications to ensure that the technology is used responsibly, ethically, and compliantly. As AI systems often make decisions or provide insights that impact individuals, businesses, and society, governance helps in building trust and transparency in how AI operates.

It ensures that AI models are:

  • Fair and unbiased, minimising discrimination or harmful outcomes.
  • Explainable and transparent, so stakeholders understand how decisions are made.
  • Secure and robust, protecting against data breaches or malicious manipulation.
  • Compliant with regulations, such as GDPR, HIPAA, or industry-specific standards.
  • Auditable and traceable, allowing organisations to monitor model performance, data lineage, and decision logic over time.

Without proper governance, AI applications can pose risks like ethical violations, financial liabilities, reputational damage, or regulatory penalties. Therefore, governance frameworks like IBM watsonx.governance are critical for managing the lifecycle, performance, and accountability of AI across its deployment.

 

Architecture

 

With governance made possible by IBM watsonx.governance on IBM Cloud, the architecture features an AI-enhanced application developed on SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) that integrates SAP HANA Cloud, SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP), and SAP Generative AI Hub. With CAP handling business logic, data management, and AI integration through LLM plugins and the SAP Cloud SDK, users engage with a user interface (UI) created with SAP UI5. The solution has vector-based search capabilities and securely accesses data stored in SAP HANA Cloud. AI services are orchestrated using SAP AI Core and Foundation Models hosted or built by SAP and its partners. To guarantee responsible AI use, compliance, and risk, the entire system is managed by IBM watsonx.governance and operates on SAP's Cloud Foundry Runtime. For enterprise-grade AI-driven applications, this solution provides a robust and scalable architecture. It leverages SAP’s Cloud Foundry Runtime and incorporates IBM watsonx.governance to guarantee responsible AI deployment, maintain compliance, and mitigate potential risks.

 

Business Scenarios

Using SAP CX with watsonx.gov: Integrating a conversational AI assistant within the SAP CX system offers real-time customer query resolution by leveraging historical support data and knowledge bases. Crucially, AI governance, powered by IBM watsonx.governance, plays a vital role in ensuring the integrity and trustworthiness of these AI interactions. It actively prevents the leakage of sensitive customer data, detects and rectifies bias or inaccuracies in AI responses, and maintains a complete audit trail of all conversations for compliance with regulations like GDPR. Continuous monitoring of model performance further guarantees the quality and fairness of the AI's assistance. This robust governance framework is paramount in customer service, where even isolated instances of incorrect or inappropriate AI responses can severely damage customer trust and lead to legal repercussions. By mitigating these risks, governance simultaneously enhances the overall customer experience.

SAP ARIBA with watsonx.gov: Leveraging data from both SAP Ariba and S/4HANA, an AI-powered assistant empowers procurement officers to create optimised purchase requisitions by intelligently analysing historical pricing, vendor performance, and prevailing market trends. To ensure the reliability and integrity of these AI-driven recommendations, robust governance is essential. This governance framework guarantees the explainability of vendor selection logic, validates the trustworthiness and timeliness of underlying data sources, and actively detects and mitigates bias to ensure fair vendor consideration. Furthermore, it ensures strict adherence to established procurement policies and audit requirements. This focus on governance is critical in procurement, where decisions directly influence organisational spending, regulatory compliance, and crucial vendor relationships, ultimately building confidence in automated recommendations and facilitating auditable procurement processes within the organisation.

 

SAP Plant Maintenance on HANA Cloud with watsonx.gov: Within a manufacturing environment, an AI-driven Maintenance Planner integrated with SAP Plant Maintenance and HANA Cloud proactively predicts equipment failure and autonomously generates maintenance orders by analysing historical data and real-time sensor inputs. To ensure the dependability and trustworthiness of this system, robust AI governance is paramount. This governance framework ensures that predictive models are trained on valid and diverse datasets, thereby minimising both false positive and negative predictions. It also continuously monitors for model drift, triggering necessary retraining to maintain reliability over time. Furthermore, every AI-driven decision is meticulously tracked, providing essential traceability for maintenance engineers and auditors within our Ahmedabad operations. Ultimately, this governance prevents both the costly consequences of over-maintenance or unnecessary shutdowns resulting from flawed AI predictions, and the severe risks associated with equipment failure. By ensuring reliable and responsible AI decision-making, it safeguards our manufacturing uptime and efficiency.

 

Conclusion

As enterprises embrace AI to enhance operations across procurement, maintenance, customer service, and finance, the integration of SAP BTP with generative AI capabilities brings immense potential for innovation and efficiency. However, this innovation must be anchored in trust, transparency, and responsibility.

By embedding IBM watsonx.governance into the SAP BTP landscape, organizations can confidently deploy AI solutions that are auditable, explainable, and compliant. It ensures that every AI model—from predictive maintenance engines to procurement advisors—follows ethical AI practices, protects sensitive data, and remains aligned with regulatory standards.

This architecture not only empowers SAP applications with next-generation intelligence but also addresses the real-world risks of ungoverned AI. Businesses can now scale their AI use cases with confidence and accountability, knowing that the technology behind their decisions is both powerful and principled. In the age of AI, governance isn’t optional—it’s essential.

To learn more about SAP BTP and IBM watsonx.gov, please visit the following resources:

  • SAP BTP Help
  • IBM watsonx.gov

entry.title: Re: Perfect Analogy to understand PI/PO Design Objects-ESR.
entry.content: this real time instance is really understandable, even difficult to forget.

entry.title: Re: BI2025: Side-by-Side Migration vs. In-Place Update.
entry.content:
The issue is always in details 🙂 and depends on customer situation and architecture. If there is requirements to change OS (like from Linux to Windows to support Crystal Reports) or change infrastructure e.g. to the cloud you have only one option to migrate content.

On other site we see also that some systems are running for many years starting from 4.2, having huge installation footprint on the disc plus some content existing in the production only and sometimes not even used. Together with the fact that some application will be deprecated in BI2025 (SAP KBA 3560025 - Which products and features are removed in SAP BusinessObjects BI platform 2025?) it could be good opportunity to perform system clean up and to optimize the architecture based on new reporting requirements.

entry.title: Re: 🇩🇪 Build AI services using the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model and SAP generative AI H
entry.content:
Dear Community Managers,

I have been able to register for this event today, but can see over 29 attendees. Could you pls confirm if participation is possible?

entry.title: SAP CCMS intergrate dingtalk
entry.content:
Hello, everyone How to send the SAP CCMS alerts to dingtalk，How to realize this requirement, thanks.

Best Regards,

hayden

entry.title: Re: How can I change growing to scroll in a Fiori element...
entry.content: I did it with script by setting the threshold of the RAP table to the required lenght. No scrolling anymore. Since the page is generated, we did not extend the page. But thanks anyway for your answer!

entry.title: Build process automation subscription failed in trial account
entry.content: I am trying to subscribe to the free trial of build process automation in my BTP trial account  for region US East (VA) but the subscription is failing.
